headline: Bilaterals III in parliamentary deliberation; committee recommends constitutional anchoring.
summary: The Federal Council's dispatch on the Bilaterals III package — signed by President Guy Parmelin and the European Commission President on 2 March 2026 — reached Parliament on 13 March and is now in full committee deliberation.

headline: Section 232 pharma tariffs land on Switzerland's biggest export sector; Federal Council's trade-deal push intensifies.
summary: President Trump's April 2 proclamation imposing Section 232 tariffs on patented pharmaceutical imports takes effect July 31, 2026, in a tiered structure based on country of origin, Commerce-approved onshoring plans, and HHS MFN-pricing agreements.

headline: NCSC NCS implementation report presented to Federal Council May 20.
summary: The Swiss National Cyber Security Centre's second report on the National Cyberstrategy (NCS) was presented at the Federal Council's 20 May meeting.

headline: 14,550 signatures against 40-antenna Starlink array in Leuk, Valais.
summary: Opponents of a planned installation of 40 Starlink satellite ground antennas in Leuk, canton Valais, presented a petition with 14,550 signatures to the Swiss government in Bern urging the project to be halted.

headline: Switzerland increasingly a prime cyber-crime target; experts flag financial and multilateral exposure.
summary: Analysis published May 20 by The Local Switzerland, drawing on expert commentary and NCSC data, highlights that Geneva's role hosting UN, ICRC, and WTO alongside Zurich's position as a global financial hub create a high-value, geographically concentrated attack surface.

headline: Russia fires Oreshnik hypersonic missile at Kyiv — 4 dead, 60+ wounded; third use of nuclear-capable weapon.
summary: Russia struck Kyiv and surrounding regions on May 24 in one of the most complex multi-vector attacks on the capital since the full-scale invasion began, deploying Oreshnik hypersonic ballistic missiles (its third confirmed use in the war), Kinzhal air-launched ballistic missiles, Iskander cruise mis

headline: Iran IRGC threatens to extend war "beyond the region" as Strait of Hormuz limps at 5% of normal traffic.
summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps warned on May 20 it would extend the conflict beyond the Middle East if the United States and Israel resume military operations against Iran.

headline: Gaza: UN Security Council warned of "permanent limbo"; aid flows at 14% of pre-war weekly average.
summary: Nickolay Mladenov, UN High Representative for Gaza, told the Security Council on May 24 that the international transition plan is paralysed — Hamas is refusing to disarm, implementation is stalled, and nearly one million people still need urgent shelter.

headline: Trump-Xi Beijing summit: "strategic stability" agreed — defined differently by each side.
summary: The May 14 Trump-Xi summit in Beijing produced significant deliverables — $17B/year in Chinese agricultural purchases (2026–2028), an initial order of 200 Boeing aircraft, and the establishment of a bilateral Board of Trade and Board of Investment — but NPR and CFR post-summit analyses (published Ma

headline: India tests MIRV Agni V as India-Pakistan strategic freeze deepens.
summary: India conducted a test of its Multiple Independent Reentry Vehicle (MIRV)-capable Agni V ICBM in May 2026, underscoring the acceleration of its strategic modernisation programme.

headline: CVE-2026-20182 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN, CVSS 10.0, active exploitation by UAT-8616; federal patch deadline May 17 (passed).
summary: A maximum-severity authentication bypass (CWE-287) in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to send a single crafted CHALLENGE_ACK message over UDP port 12346 (DTLS).

headline: CVE-2026-42897 — Microsoft Exchange OWA stored XSS, CVSS 8.1, active exploitation; federal deadline May 29.
summary: Disclosed May 14, CVE-2026-42897 is a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Exchange Server Outlook Web Access affecting on-premises versions: 2016, 2019, and Subscription Edition (Exchange Online is not affected).

headline: Laravel-Lang supply chain: 700+ poisoned Composer versions exfiltrate cloud keys, CI tokens, crypto wallets.
summary: Detected May 22, 2026, by Aikido Security: attackers who had gained write access to the laravel-lang GitHub organisation rewrote every git tag across three widely used Composer localisation packages (laravel-lang/http-statuses, laravel-lang/actions, laravel-lang/attributes) within a 15-minute window

headline: CISA KEV May 21: Langflow CVE-2025-34291 (CVSS 9.4) and Trend Micro Apex One CVE-2026-34926 (CVSS 6.7); federal deadline June 4.
summary: CISA added two actively exploited flaws to the KEV catalog on May 21, 2026.

headline: Project Glasswing update (May 22): Anthropic Mythos AI flags 10,000+ critical bugs in open source; patching rate lags badly.
summary: Anthropic's Project Glasswing — launched April 7, 2026, powered by the unreleased Claude Mythos Preview frontier model — published a running update on May 22: approximately 50 trusted partner teams have collectively identified 10,000+ high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across systemically im
